headline: Johor-Singapore SEZ Promises 100,000 Jobs Over 10 Years
context: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone officially launched in January 2026, offering tax rates as low as 5% to attract RM120 billion in investments and create 100,000 high-income jobs over a decade.

cards.big: JS-SEZ offers 5% tax rate — but Malaysia's standard 24% corporate tax loses RM4.2 billion if firms relocate within country
cards.sub: Penang and Selangor fear companies moving south to exploit Johor's SEZ tax arbitrage

cards.big: Previous Iskandar Malaysia SEZ delivered only 42% of its 2006-2025 job creation target of 860,000 positions
cards.sub: RM88 billion in Iskandar tax incentives yielded mainly property speculation, not industrial development

cards.big: JS-SEZ targets high-skilled jobs paying RM8,000+ but Johor's median household income is only RM6,800
cards.sub: Risk of enclave economy where SEZ workers are imported while local Johoreans remain in lower-wage sectors

cards.big: SEZ governance split between IRDA, MIDA, and Singapore's EDB with no single accountability framework
cards.sub: Three-country-two-system model creates jurisdictional grey zones for labour rights and tax compliance

cards.big: Malaysia has created 17 economic zones since 1990 — adding another one doesn't fix why the previous ones underperformed
Should JS-SEZ tax incentives be conditional on hiring 70% local Johor workforce to prevent enclave dynamics?
